verdict_reasoning: HSPA5/GRP78/BiP is canonically an ER-lumenal chaperone with no TM domain or GPI anchor. However, a well-documented cancer-cell ecto-pool exists: tumor cells display GRP78 on the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane, where it acts as a co-receptor for various ligands, a target for anti-tumor antibodies, and has been exploited by ADC and immunotherapy programs. This surface display is not observed at meaningful levels on normal somatic cells. Additionally, during ER stress (viral infection, hypoxia, tumorigenesis) GRP78 translocates to the cell surface. Cell-death-induced externalization is also documented. These qualify as cell_state_induced contextual surface accessibility. The protein itself has no inherent membrane anchor, but the cancer/stress ecto-pool is well-established and therapeutically exploited.
